source_file: 4-5.FL.2 Handout B. SMART Goal Brainstorm and Planning.pdf
output_file: 4-5.FL.2 Handout B. SMART Goal Brainstorm and Planning.md

analysis.summary: This is a student worksheet for grades 4-5 financial literacy that teaches students to create and refine personal goals using the SMART goals framework. Students first identify short-term and lifetime goals, then transform them into specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-based objectives.

entities.name: SMART Goals Framework
entities.description: Goal-setting methodology with five criteria: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-based

entities.name: Grades 4-5 Financial Literacy Lesson 2
entities.description: The second lesson in a financial literacy curriculum designed for elementary students in grades 4 and 5

entities.name: SMART Goal Brainstorm and Planning
entities.description: Student handout worksheet for practicing SMART goal creation

entities.name: School Year Goal
entities.description: Short-term personal goal to be accomplished within one academic year

entities.name: Lifetime Goal
entities.description: Long-term personal goal to be accomplished over one's lifetime

entities.name: Specific Criteria
entities.description: SMART goal component requiring clear, well-defined objectives

entities.name: Measurable Criteria
entities.description: SMART goal component requiring quantifiable or trackable outcomes

entities.name: Achievable Criteria
entities.description: SMART goal component ensuring goals are realistic and attainable

entities.name: Relevant Criteria
entities.description: SMART goal component ensuring goals are meaningful and worthwhile

entities.name: Time-based Criteria
entities.description: SMART goal component requiring specific deadlines or timeframes

entities.name: Reading Goal Example
entities.description: Sample demonstration showing how to convert 'read more books' into a SMART goal

relationships.context: This worksheet is Handout B for the second financial literacy lesson
Short-term goals are converted using the SMART criteria
Long-term goals are converted using the SMART criteria
Specific is the first component of SMART goals
Measurable is the second component of SMART goals
Achievable is the third component of SMART goals
Relevant is the fourth component of SMART goals
Time-based is the fifth component of SMART goals
The example demonstrates how to apply all five SMART criteria to transform a vague goal
